verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: “The next question we have to ask,” says he, “is this: If man cannot be without sin, whose fault is it,—man’s own, or some one’s else?

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: If man’s own, in what way is it his fault if he is not that which he is unable to be?”

verses.book: A Treatise Concerning Man’s Perfection in Righteousness.
verses.text: We reply, that it is man’s fault that he is not without sin on this account, because it has by man’s sole will come to pass that he has come into such a necessity as cannot be overcome by man’s sole will.
